filed.title: [REVIEW] Cross-Origin Policies: scored by presence only — `unsafe-none` / `cross-origin` get full credit
filed.finding: checkCrossOriginPolicies in src/rules.ts:145-164 scores COOP/COEP/CORP by presence only, awarding full 5/5 'good' status to the most permissive opt-out values (COOP: unsafe-none, COEP: unsafe-none, CORP: cross-origin) with no findings or recommendations.

filed.title: [REVIEW] CSP: Content-Security-Policy-Report-Only is invisible to the analyzer — report-only deployments score 0/30 as if no CSP exists
filed.finding: checkCSP in src/rules.ts:42 only queries 'content-security-policy', never 'content-security-policy-report-only'; sites in the standard report-only rollout phase score 0/30 with status 'missing' and a misleading recommendation to 'add a CSP' even though one is already deployed.

runner_ups.finding: CSP wildcard regex /(?:default-src|script-src)\s+\*/i only matches when '*' is the first source token; 'default-src 'self' *' is not flagged.
runner_ups.reason_not_filed: lower score; now covered by open issue #16

runner_ups.finding: checkCSP does not recognize Content-Security-Policy-Report-Only header; report-only deployments are treated as if no CSP exists.
runner_ups.reason_not_filed: previously runner-up; filed this run as issue #20 with refined scoring

runner_ups.finding: HSTS check awards full credit (preload bonus) when 'preload' directive is present even if max-age < 63072000 (2 years), which is the minimum the actual hstspreload.org submission requires.
runner_ups.reason_not_filed: lower score; addressed in part by open issue #17

runner_ups.finding: Referrer-Policy classifies 'no-referrer-when-downgrade' as a strong value (score 10), but it is the historical default and is widely considered weak for cross-origin URL leakage.
fetchHeaders uses method: 'HEAD' which causes false-missing readings on sites that set security headers conditionally on GET (e.g., HTML-only middleware).
checkCSP does not flag missing base-uri directive, leaving <base> injection silently bypassing script-src 'self'.
runner_ups.reason_not_filed: previously duplicate of open issue #5; #5 now closed

runner_ups.finding: Permissions-Policy tests in test/analyzer.test.ts (lines 276-293 and 341-355) assert old permissive scoring behavior, conflicting with the strict scoring added in commit 8d29a8c; 4 tests fail on HEAD — CI is broken.
runner_ups.reason_not_filed: duplicate of open issue #15 (filed by a different review run earlier today)

runner_ups.finding: getVersion() in src/cli.ts:20-26 uses require('../package.json') in an ESM module (package.json type:module); require throws ReferenceError in ESM, catch returns '0.0.0'; --version and --help always display version 0.0.0.
runner_ups.reason_not_filed: lower score than CSP-Report-Only; DX bug but not a security or correctness issue in header analysis

clean_areas: package.json exports/main/types/bin fields are correctly configured
CI workflows (ci.yml, publish.yml, auto-tag.yml) are sound
X-Frame-Options check (rules.ts:73-91) — accepts DENY/SAMEORIGIN and CSP frame-ancestors fallback as expected
